_meta.description: SR paper library — Nae Myo Aung Khing, ESML Doctoral Batch 5
_meta.topic: TP² Framework: Neurodivergent Learners in Higher Education
_meta.note: Commit this file to GitHub to share the library across devices. Export updated versions from SR App → Library → Export papers.json

papers.title: Art, Design and Neurodiversity
papers.author: Damiani, M.
papers.essence: Explores how art and design education intersects with neurodivergent student experiences and creative expression.

papers.title: Inclusive Education in Thailand: Environmental Design and Planning
papers.author: Tantixalerm et al.
papers.journal: Nakhara: Journal of Environmental Design and Planning
papers.essence: Examines inclusive education models in Thailand with focus on environmental and physical design for learning spaces.
papers.keywords: Executive Function Support, Multimodal Learning Access, Psychological Safety, Student Support System, Engagement

papers.title: Music Education for Students with Autism Spectrum Disorder in a Full Inclusion Context
papers.author: Draper, E.
papers.journal: Journal of Research in Music Education
papers.essence: Investigates how music education in full-inclusion settings supports ASD students' engagement, creative expression, and social participation.
papers.keywords: Executive Function Support, Multimodal Learning Access, Mentorship / Responsive Leadership, Performance, Engagement

papers.title: Reframing Neurodiversity in Engineering Education
papers.author: Chrysochoou, M. et al.
papers.essence: Conceptual analysis reframing neurodiversity as an asset in engineering education, proposing inclusive curriculum and assessment adaptations.
papers.keywords: Executive Function Support, Multimodal Learning Access, Psychological Safety, Self-Determined Learning / Heutagogy, Curriculum Flexibility

papers.title: Diversified: Promoting Co-production in Course Design
papers.author: Cumming, T. et al.
papers.essence: Presents a co-production framework for inclusive course design that centres neurodivergent student voices in curriculum development.
papers.keywords: Executive Function Support, Multimodal Learning Access, Mentorship / Responsive Leadership, Curriculum Flexibility, Performance

papers.title: Supporting Neurodivergent Learners in Higher Education (Frontiers in Education, 2025)
papers.author: Unknown Author(s)
papers.essence: Original research on neurodivergent learner support in higher education contexts. Full author details to be confirmed.
papers.keywords: Executive Function Support, Multimodal Learning Access, Psychological Safety, Curriculum Flexibility, Performance

papers.title: Compassionate Pedagogy for Neurodivergent Learners
papers.author: Hamilton, L. & Petty, S.
papers.essence: Explores compassionate pedagogical approaches tailored for neurodivergent students, emphasising psychological safety, trust, and relational teaching.
papers.keywords: Executive Function Support, Multimodal Learning Access, Psychological Safety, Self-Determined Learning / Heutagogy, Student Support System

papers.title: A Systematic Review of Actions Aimed at Improving Attention and Behaviour in ADHD University Students
papers.author: Álvarez-Godos, J. et al.
papers.essence: Systematic review of interventions targeting attention and behavioural outcomes for ADHD students in university settings.
papers.keywords: Executive Function Support, Self-Determined Learning / Heutagogy, Mentorship / Responsive Leadership, Curriculum Flexibility, Student Support System

papers.title: A Rapid Review of Supports for Neurodivergent Students in Higher Education
papers.author: McDowall, A. & Kiseleva, M.
papers.essence: Rapid review synthesising institutional and academic supports for neurodivergent students, with implications for policy and practice.
papers.keywords: Executive Function Support, Psychological Safety, Mentorship / Responsive Leadership, Curriculum Flexibility, Student Support System

papers.title: Musical Creativity in Autism: Exploring Growth through Collaborative Peer Interaction
papers.author: Johnson, C.M. & LaGasse, A.B.
papers.journal: International Journal of Disability, Development and Education
papers.essence: Examines how collaborative peer music-making fosters creative growth, engagement, and psychosocial outcomes in autistic learners.
papers.keywords: Executive Function Support, Mentorship / Responsive Leadership, Engagement

papers.title: Neurodiversity in Higher Education: A Narrative Synthesis
papers.author: Clouder, L. et al.
papers.essence: Narrative synthesis of neurodiversity research in higher education, mapping support structures, challenges, and outcomes across multiple studies.
papers.keywords: Executive Function Support, Multimodal Learning Access, Psychological Safety, Mentorship / Responsive Leadership, Student Support System

papers.title: Heutagogy for Neurodivergent Learners in Higher Education
papers.author: Friedman, B. & Nash-Luckenbach, D.
papers.essence: Applies heutagogical principles to neurodivergent learner contexts, arguing for self-determined, flexible learning environments in HE.
papers.keywords: Executive Function Support, Multimodal Learning Access, Self-Determined Learning / Heutagogy, Curriculum Flexibility, Engagement

papers.title: Systematic Review: HE Interventions for Neurodivergent Students' Mental Health
papers.author: Ross, J. et al.
papers.essence: Systematic review of higher education interventions targeting the mental health and wellbeing of neurodivergent students.
papers.keywords: Executive Function Support, Psychological Safety, Mentorship / Responsive Leadership, Student Support System, Engagement

papers.title: Shaping Leadership and Inclusive Education: Insights from Cambodia, Myanmar, and Vietnam
papers.author: Oo, M.Y. et al.
papers.essence: Explores how school leadership shapes inclusive education practices across Cambodia, Myanmar, and Vietnam in developing contexts.
papers.keywords: Executive Function Support, Psychological Safety, Curriculum Flexibility, Engagement

papers.title: The Effectiveness of Universal Design for Learning: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
papers.author: Almeqdad, Q.I. et al.
papers.essence: Systematic review and meta-analysis evaluating the effectiveness of Universal Design for Learning (UDL) across educational settings.
papers.keywords: Executive Function Support, Multimodal Learning Access, Performance, Engagement

papers.title: Uncovering Employment Outcomes for Autistic University Graduates in the United Kingdom
papers.author: Vincent, J. & Ralston, K.
papers.essence: Analyses employment outcomes for autistic graduates in the UK, revealing persistent underemployment and the role of university support.
papers.keywords: Executive Function Support, Self-Determined Learning / Heutagogy, Mentorship / Responsive Leadership, Engagement, Innovation Readiness
